title: PCR protocol for MgSTS marker genotyping
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3uug2","text":"This is a standard PCR protocol used to amplify MgSTS markers from genomic DNA for genotyping. MgSTS markers were developed for use in Mimulus species and typically span length polymorphisms between conserved sequence regions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"c247l","text":"Taq polymerase and any mix containing it should be kept on ice at all times. Keep GoTaq enzyme stock in the -20C freezer except when loading. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Determine the amount of reagents needed. The following amounts are sufficient for a single reaction: 2.95 µL, Molecular Biology Grade Water 2 µL, 5X GoTaq Flexi Buffer, colorless 0.8 µL, MgCl2, 25mM stock 0.8 µL, dNTPs, 2.5mM stock 0.5 µL, BSA, 10 mg/mL stock .2 µL, Premixed F+R Primers, 5uM stock 0.15 µL, GoTaq G2 polymerase, 5 units/uL stock 2 µL, DNA sample Note: Markers that are known to amplify in different, non-overlapping length ranges can be combined into a single PCR reaction. Add 0.2 µL of EACH premixed primer pair, and reduce your water volume accordingly so that the final reaction volume is 10 µL .
Create a master mix with all ingredients except DNA. Add the polymerase last, mix well, and keep the mix on ice.

Run the following PCR cycler program: 94 °C, 2 mins 00 sec 10 cycles of stepping down annealing temp: > 94 °C, 0 mins 30 sec > 62 °C, 0 mins 45 sec : Reduced by 1C every cycle to 53 °C > 72 °C, 1 min 00 sec 35 cycles of constant annealing temp: > 94 °C, 0 mins 30 sec > 52 °C, 0 mins 45 sec > 72 °C, 1 min 00 sec 72 °C, 10 mins 10 °C, Hold

Once the PCR is complete, prepare the following reaction in a new plate: HiDi and ROX size standard can be premixed and aliquoted beforehand. For marker lengths > 500, use ROX1000. For smaller lengths, ROX500 can be used. 8.25 µL, HiDi buffer 0.25 µL, ROX size standard 1.5 µL, PCR sample
Briefly spin down the plate to remove air bubbles and sample stuck to the sides. Then cover with foil PCR covers and submit to your sequencing provider.

Transfer 8 µL of master mix to each well of a PCR plate. Then add 2 µL sample DNA to each well. Add a plastic seal to the plate and press all edges to ensure the seal is tight. Mix well, spin the plate to collect droplets, and place in PCR machine.
Thaw mix components except for GoTaq polymerase. Leave the polymerase in the freezer until it is needed.
creator: Matthew Farnitano
